title: Molluscum contagiosum review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

assessment.text: Supports recording pediatric or adult context, lesion number and size, immunosuppression, and secondary bacterial infection concern without inferring a complication.
assessment.rationale: The risk-context section supports recording lesion burden, immunosuppression and secondary infection concern only when assessed; no complication or sexual-transmission conclusion is generated.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports recording pediatric or adult context, lesion number and size, immunosuppression, and secondary bacterial infection concern without inferring a complication.

exact_section.heading: Key points and clinical-features risk context
exact_section.locator: official HCP page, Key points and Clinical features

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting small pearly papules, central depression, approximate lesion size, pain status, inflammation, redness, swelling, and interval course.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact CDC clinical-features section supports documenting an established or suspected molluscum review, lesion course, symptoms, inflammation, redness, swelling, site and distribution without inferring diagnosis or treatment need.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting small pearly papules, central depression, approximate lesion size, pain status, inflammation, redness, swelling, and interval course.

exact_section.heading: Clinical features
exact_section.locator: official HCP page, Clinical features section

sources_used.title: Clinical Overview of Molluscum Contagiosum
sources_used.jurisdiction: United States; international infectious-dermatology evidence requiring UAE adaptation

additions.text: Supports documenting small pearly papules, central depression, approximate lesion size, pain status, inflammation, redness, swelling, and interval course.
additions.rationale: The exact CDC clinical-features section supports documenting an established or suspected molluscum review, lesion course, symptoms, inflammation, redness, swelling, site and distribution without inferring diagnosis or treatment need.
additions.evidence_extract: Supports documenting small pearly papules, central depression, approximate lesion size, pain status, inflammation, redness, swelling, and interval course.

source_limitations: Exact lesion number, size and morphology, affected sites, eczema, secondary infection, immune status, sexual-health and safeguarding context, diagnosis, treatment, procedure, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
